clusters.label: A
clusters.summary: The San Gabriel Mountains are experiencing extreme fire risk with 37 hexagons at VERY_HIGH risk, averaging 30.54% probability of fire. The area is under intense heat stress with max temperatures reaching 106.6°F and sustained high temperatures over the past 7 days. The model's top drivers include geographic location (historically fire-prone area), satellite daytime surface temperature (44.4°C), and cumulative 30-day precipitation deficit. CAL FIRE is advised to pre-position Type 1 engines within 15-minute response time and have aerial resources on standby at the nearest air base.
actions: Pre-position Type 1 engines within 15-minute response time of high-risk areas.
Have aerial resources (helicopters, air tankers) on standby at the nearest air base.
Activate the CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO framework, moving to SET status, and be prepared to issue evacuation warnings.

clusters.label: B
clusters.summary: The Shasta-Trinity Region is experiencing extreme fire risk with 79 hexagons at VERY_HIGH or HIGH risk, averaging 19.21% probability of fire. The area is under intense heat stress with max temperatures reaching 104.4°F and sustained high temperatures over the past 7 days. The model's top drivers include geographic location (historically fire-prone area), cumulative 30-day precipitation deficit, and satellite daytime surface temperature (45.2°C). CAL FIRE is advised to pre-position Type 1 engines within 15-minute response time and have aerial resources on standby at the nearest air base.

clusters.label: C
clusters.summary: The Lake Tahoe / El Dorado area is experiencing high fire risk with 4 hexagons at HIGH risk, averaging 9.96% probability of fire. The area is under heat stress with max temperatures reaching 113.7°F. The model's top drivers include geographic location (historically fire-prone area), geographic location (longitude — historically fire-prone area), and 16-day smoothed vegetation greenness. CAL FIRE is advised to pre-position water tenders at key highway corridors and have incident management teams on alert for potential deployment.
actions: Pre-position water tenders at key highway corridors near wildland-urban interface.

overall_assessment: California is experiencing a statewide high fire risk with 8 active risk clusters, including 93 VERY_HIGH risk hexagons. The San Gabriel Mountains and Shasta-Trinity Region are experiencing extreme fire risk, with the model's top drivers including geographic location (historically fire-prone areas) and satellite daytime surface temperature. CAL FIRE is advised to activate the READY-SET-GO framework, pre-position resources, and have incident management teams on alert for potential deployment.
